text.title: Intense 48 Hours in Beijing China 🇨🇳
text.u: arriving to China VI the Visa free
Transit rule means that we can only stay
in one Province luckily the Great Wall
of China is only an hour and a half away
from Beijing and within the capital
region there is no way we could miss
seeing one of the Seven Wonders of the
World so we booked a slot on a local
tour grabbed a bus and headed to the
Great Wall of
China every step is a free fall for
me this is one of the most incredible
places we've ever been this is just
stunning you feel like you're walking
through history this area of the W is
over 600 years old and yeah you feel the
history when you're here the mountains
surrounding this place are just
incredible I can't believe we're here
one of the Seven Wonders of the
World stunning and it's just an hour and
a half from the center of
Beijing
incredible I didn't expect to see
mountains like this and I didn't expect
to
feel the way I do
and I can't even explain how I
feel smell
water and you just feel history right
you can feel yeah how Grand it is you
can feel the scale of it mhm and um I'm
just shocked you see on left the scenery
is just mind-blowing you look on the
right and you see a wall and then you
look for the hole here and is just Jo
dropping and you can get a great
exercise walking around here I feel like
our legs are definitely going to shake
at the end of the day my legs are
shaking this morning so I'm already in
trouble oh wow that's
de apparently we're going up to the best
Viewpoint there is up here very exciting
I mean if it looks that beautiful
now I
know so the first part of the war was
originally erected in the 7th Century BC
uh 700 years BC was that the 8th Century
BC but it took 20% of the Chinese
population at that time of the Ching
Dynasty to build this wall and 10% of
the Chinese population died building
this wall so as well as being known as
The Great Wall it's also known as the
wall of
tombs waiting for us to take pictures
[Music]
so we're heading all the way up there to
the viewpoint on the 20th
Tower and from there we're going to be
over 1,000 m above sea level and the
view should be
insane we are currently in the muan Yu
section of the wall which is one of the
two places you can see the wall on a day
trip from be
but how we understand this is the more
quiet of the two and we also got here at
10:30 in the morning so there's a lot
less people and yeah to be honest I
didn't imagine that it was going to be
this empty and we were going to
basically have the entire place to
ourselves it is just
unreal it's quite unbelievable we just
talking about it to think how many
people visit this place throughout the
years it's
insane so I would have answered that
talk with you about come you talk
anymore it's getting worse it is going
to get worse before it gets
better view is going to be worth it 100%
keep telling yourselves
that everyone's just saying hi welcome
to
China

ready
yeah I'm leaning more toward

your
I think I'm G to my heart

break I had a little break let's

than just a

it is as bad as it

looks

great
we just arrived at the Temple of Heaven
and it's located in the massive Park so
we're just walking around and I think
it's located somewhere over there in the
middle we just wanted to walk around
here and then head to the temple itself
the entrance is 34 y each so you need to
purchase ticket before you get in so we
did and now we're
here
there's so many different places to go
within this park and things to see that
you could quite easily spend half a day
here just in this park and yeah when you
look at the map there's different
sections and different Gates that you
can enter and exit from and it's just
incredible I'd like to say to be honest
it's a massive change of pace from the
outside City but to be honest Beijing in
general doesn't seem like one of these
really loud really busy over the top
cities and I'm surprised to say that I
was expecting it to be exactly that when
we came here but yeah compared to let's
say Tokyo or where we've just come from
in Vietnam it's incredibly quiet and
free of traffic and uh yeah it's just a
quiet nice city to walk around in
general but anyway the park is still a
lovely place to come to soak in the
smell and just fill the
atmosphere
there is a Chinese rose garden
definitely want to go there but it's
going to smell
amazing
okay
[Music]
here is the Rose
Garden let's go and sniff
them so many
flowers I want to see those ones
first wow this so
gorgeous look at
that Matt s this one it smells so
good it smells like a candy it's like
rose sweet tea it's so good I haven't
smelled rose that beautiful before it's
unusual right yeah really tasty I'm the
sniffer in this family no gets that
excited there was a bee inside that
Rose
close call I guess this
time this is my
heaven I'm not
surprised it's lovely the smells are
incredible even from here he's get a
slight waft
yes I suggest we go have a look around
the
Temple and then go and get some food I'm
getting
pish I want a
picture I'm going
to we we usually live in
Canada okay travel nice yeah how long
are you here for for 3 weeks oh okay
yeah she's actually she's born here yeah
but we just immigrated to Canada how

years like 10 years 10 years okay it's
already home yeah yeah basically
traveling around China yeah a little bit
mostly in Beijing my family they're here
sure
mostly and you too you're also from

type from Taiwan nice oh that's amazing
sorry we won't take too much of your
time happy
to thank you thank you very much have an
amazing day have a good trip nice to
meet you nice Mee you have a good day
byebye nice to meet
you thank
you good pictures as well oh my
god look so
awkward typical find the rush bit hello
hello there's definitely certain past
the park that aren't so empty and are
definitely very very crowded but just
look at this

so
busy

wow
they so right outside of the park on the
East Gate there is a market which is
called Hong Chow market and it's also
known as the fake market and it's here
in Hong Chow that you're supposed to be
able to find anything fake that you want
from fake Rolexes to fake clothes to
anything else you can imagine so uh yeah
let's go and take a look and um see if
the rooms are
true
hope the food is not
fake otherwise everyone's in
trouble okay the market will have to
wait because we smell something amazing
and this is a certain type of chicken
noodles from Xian we think but yeah they
look incredible and smell even better so
uh yeah onapp tea we'll go to the market
in a minute you tried it
[Music]
already really good mhm the noodles are
so
big
wow we were just saying how interesting
it is that these big types of noodles
are so similar to different types of
Italian pasta that you get and when you
think that xan was the start or the
finish of the ancient Silk Road and
Venice was the other end it's
interesting to see how this sort of dish
a noodles or pasta depending on what you
call it has been passed along and the
Traditions are so similar even here in
China there's so many similarities to
European Cuisine amazing so
good I find incredibly
interesting
AR like um Traditions that have been
pass along on Sil Ro is just incredible
you were saying how similar this is Tob
L man yeah like it tastes nothing like
Lan but you have your past you have your
meat you have carrots potatoes so the
ingredients are similar and I find it
fascinating cuz I love food I like
tracing all these steps down affects the
culture how people cook what they eat
you know spices they use yeah different
ingredients different spices but the
traditions and the methods are so
similar despite thousands of miles of
distance that is better let's head to
the
market do you it's
real
you want to buy
t-shirt don't shut me off
this is so typically China to be honest
when we heard about this Market we
thought it was going to be like an

seeing certain Soviet countries maybe in
a like a some sort of factory looking
building but this is like a modern
shopping mall basically isn't it this is
no different to nbk in

that's what I mean when you look at like
the technical stuff it all looks like
real if I W we weren't told in advance
it was

little flavor this is yeah this is the
the technical themed floor it seems with
all sorts of iPhones even your brand new
pnot 15
proit nuty my
God I spent three months speaking
Russian more than

than life itself makes a wonderful

floor Nailed It smash
it oh on the Fab Flor now
they know how to get

really I think you put like two pcks in
there or

which means
what absolutely of course he does that's

of we were told this we told this is the

is it

downstairs and like when you look at all

maybe it's

chandelier what is a picture of
the one that doesn't light

go so to be honest that was all kind of
civilized that wasn't quite what we were
expecting did I think we're all
expecting sort of more of a a cattle
market Vibe with people shouting at you
from Left Right and center and just yeah
lots of people around lots of bargaining
and there was a little bit of that going
on but it was all very civilized but
there still was a lot of different
things you could buy from clothes to
technical equipment to jewelry I think
it's a nice place if you want to buy
something yeah but we got the point of
what it looks like now we want to head
out somewhere random just out inside the
city center just have a look around what
like an Normal area looks
like and we did buy one thing by the way
we bought some uh Chinese hamburger hope
not F hopefully it is actually
meat give you

bless the underground here is really

just I diding I'm
agree I
so we came off at a station called dong

just to have a look at a more sort of
lived in less touristy area of Beijing
not really sure if this is or not as I
say we just picked it at random but so
far it looks it it looks a lot quieter
and the buildings are a lot smaller and
quaint than in the very center itself
and uh yeah we're just going to have a
little walk around and have a look at
normal daily life here in Beijing going
[Music]
by
so I mentioned in the last video um here
in Beijing about how many bikes there
are in the city and that's true but
there's also a lot of scooters there's
in mopeds just like they were in Vietnam
but the big difference is between here
and Vietnam is unlike the gas guzzle as
there were here most of the mopeds here
in Beijing are like this electric mopeds
which is obviously amazing really good
for the environment and but the only
issue with that is they are so quiet you
can't hear them so when you're crossing
the road or anything like that these
mopeds come at you out of nowhere and
I'm just literally crossing the road had
to sweep right across me and you can't
hear them at all until the last moment
until they Lally whiz out in front of
you wow look at that building W it's
like um train station it's like some

uhhuh
this is definitely one of our favorite
things to do even in a city like this
even in a massive Megapolis you can
always find little areas where you can
get away from the hustle and bustle
where you can see what life used to be
like and um yeah people are so so
friendly even when you just walk down
their random street holding a GoPro
everyone says hello everyone's
smiling love
it I'm back into the
city
never too
far good morning from Beijing Capital
airport the fact we're here means
obviously this amazing few days in China
have come to an end and to be honest
these few days have left us absolutely
captivated by this country and
Incredibly incredibly excited to come
back and explore different regions
different areas different cities so so
interested 3 days is all we needed to be
absolutely captivated by China but yeah
now it's time to move on so thank you
very much for watching this video and
the last one from China and um see you
in the next
one oh my God there's so much more
you want to take it and
film I don't think anyone cares it but
